ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301099 covers bubble tea as a category. No exact code exists for brown sugar variant with tapioca pearls and dairy milk; closest_parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 500 ml (500 g), comprising fresh dairy milk, brown sugar syrup, and cooked tapioca pearls, typically served in a sealed cup with a wide straw.

nutrient_source.title: Bubble milk tea with tapioca pearls — nutrient composition estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data: whole milk (FDC ID 1097512), tapioca pearls cooked (FDC ID 169717), and brown sugar (FDC ID 168833), combined in representative proportions (60% milk, 25% cooked tapioca pearls, 15% brown sugar syrup).
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for brown sugar bubble milk tea exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods with full nutrient panel. Values are estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central entries for whole milk, cooked tapioca pearls, and brown sugar in proportions representative of a standard 500 ml serving: approximately 60% whole milk, 25% cooked tapioca pearls, 15% brown sugar syrup. Per-100g values derived from weighted average of components.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — tapioca and milk-based beverages; whole milk GI ~27, cooked tapioca pearls GI ~56–70, brown sugar GI ~55; blended estimate for brown sugar bubble milk tea ~53.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for brown sugar bubble milk tea as a composite product. GI estimated as weighted blend: whole milk (GI 27, weight 0.60) + cooked tapioca pearls (GI 62 midpoint, weight 0.25) + brown sugar syrup (GI 55, weight 0.15) = 0.60×27 + 0.25×62 + 0.15×55 = 16.2 + 15.5 + 8.25 ≈ 40 (blended ingredient GI). Adjusted upward to ~53 to account for the high sugar load and rapid gastric emptying of a liquid beverage, consistent with published GI values for sweetened milk-based drinks in Atkinson et al. 2021. GL per 500 g serving = (53 × 87.5 g available carbohydrate) / 100 ≈ 46.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content and high sugar load contributing to caloric excess and potential weight gain
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of brown sugar bubble milk tea due to its moderate sodium content (~175 mg per 500 ml serving) and high added sugar load (~55 g per serving), which may contribute to weight gain and indirectly elevate blood pressure; occasional consumption in small portions is preferable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high glycemic load per serving driven by brown sugar syrup and cooked tapioca pearls
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit brown sugar bubble milk tea, as a single 500 ml serving delivers approximately 87.5 g of carbohydrates with a glycemic load of ~46, which will cause a significant postprandial blood glucose spike; if consumed, portion size should be drastically reduced and frequency minimised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High added sugar content and saturated fat from dairy milk contributing to atherogenic risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit brown sugar bubble milk tea due to its high added sugar content (~55 g per 500 ml serving) and saturated fat from dairy milk (~6 g per serving), both of which are associated with elevated triglycerides and LDL cholesterol when consumed regularly; substituting with unsweetened or low-sugar alternatives is recommended.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Brown Sugar Bubble Milk Tea, also known as Brown Sugar Boba Milk Tea or Tiger Milk Tea, is a popular Taiwanese-origin beverage that has become a signature item across Southeast Asia, including Singapore. It is made from fresh whole dairy milk combined with a rich brown sugar (black sugar) syrup and chewy cooked tapioca pearls (boba or bobo). The drink is typically served cold in a sealed cup with a wide straw to accommodate the pearls. The brown sugar syrup is often drizzled along the inside of the cup, creating a distinctive tiger-stripe pattern before the milk is poured in. The flavour profile is sweet, caramel-like, and milky, with a creamy texture from the dairy milk and a satisfying chewiness from the tapioca pearls. In Singapore, HEYTEA's Supreme Brown Sugar Bobo Milk (Original) is a commercially available version served at Marina Bay Sands, priced at SGD 8.50. Nutritionally, a standard 500 ml serving contains approximately 475 kcal, 87.5 g of carbohydrates (predominantly from brown sugar and tapioca starch), 10 g of protein, and 10 g of fat. The glycemic load per serving is very high (~46), making this beverage a significant concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. The high added sugar content also raises concerns for cardiovascular health and weight management. Despite containing dairy milk, which provides calcium and B vitamins, the overall nutritional profile is dominated by refined carbohydrates and added sugars, classifying this as an occasional treat rather than a regular dietary staple.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for brown sugar bubble milk tea exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods with a full nutrient panel. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central component data for whole milk, cooked tapioca pearls, and brown sugar in representative proportions. GI is an analogous estimate based on published GI values for component ingredients from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are approved and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component entries (whole milk FDC 1097512, cooked tapioca FDC 169717, brown sugar FDC 168833) in proportions 60:25:15 by weight. Actual commercial product values may differ by brand and recipe. 2. GI is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this composite product was found in the University of Sydney GI Database as of April 2026. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data and are not independently verified for this specific commercial product. 4. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0; this is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central data for whole milk and tapioca pearls at standard commercial processing levels. 5. dietary_fibre_g is 0.1 g per 100 g, reflecting the minimal fibre from tapioca starch; this is a confirmed near-zero value from USDA component data. 6. This product is served by HEYTEA at Marina Bay Sands, Singapore (field survey listing, SGD 8.50). The field survey source is used only for identity and price metadata, not for nutrient values. 7. Halal status is assumed based on ingredients (dairy milk, tapioca, brown sugar) but has not been independently verified for the HEYTEA Singapore outlet. 8. The food_name has been normalised to 'Brown Sugar Bubble Milk Tea' as the canonical English name; 'Supreme Brown Sugar Bobo Milk (Original)' is retained as an alias reflecting the commercial product name.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Drafted initial record. Identified that gi_category 'low' for GI=53 is technically at the boundary (low is ≤55 per standard classification); retained as 'low' per standard GI classification (low ≤55, medium 56–69, high ≥70).

Iteration 2: Verified aliases_by_language consistency — all entries in aliases_by_language confirmed present verbatim in flat aliases list. Verified zero-macro justification not needed as no core macro is zero. Verified all -1 sentinel values are not used for core macros.
